subject: SOCDS 2025/26 Update Week #27 - PreK Orange
text:
Email Ms. Annemarie
Pet Care Donations
Support the Humane Society
PEP is collecting donations for the Associated Humane Society – Newark Shelter (
https://ahscares.org/newark/
) from
Monday, March 16 through Friday, March 20
. Please drop off unopened, unexpired dog, cat, or kitten food, as well as clean blankets and towels, in the donation bins outside of SOCDS.
The shelter recently launched an exciting new program called Adventure Tails, where volunteers can take a dog out of the shelter for a few hours. If you'd like to sign up, you can do so here
https://www.shelterluv.com/form/volunteer/AHNJ/79774-adventure-tails-program-interest-form
.
If you're interested in helping transport donations from the SOMA community to the shelter in the future, please contact Sara Paddock directly at
sara.paddock@gmail.com
. They’re always looking for extra hands!
Five in a Row
Our books this month celebrate a variety of spring or renewal themes.
Ramadan Moon
This story introduces children to the month of Ramadan and the relationship to the moon.
Leprechaun's Treasure
As we build Leprechaun traps, this story emphasizes kindness, empathy, and the meaning of luck.
Festival of Colors
This book follows a family through their preparations for Holi and the use of color to spread spring joy and hope.
I am Love
This book encourages children to sit with their upset and look inward with love and affection for themselves and others.
Crazy Hat Day
Monday March 16th
On Monday your children are planning to wear crowns, headbands, clown hats, and all kinds of other headgear.  This is in honor of Dr. Seuss'
The Cat in the Hat.
If you have any questions or concerns please text (1-646-226-8790) or email learn@socds.com.
Annemarie
Upcoming Dates
Monday, March 16th - Crazy Hat Day
Week of March 16th - Humane Society
Pet Supply Drive
Thursday, March 19th - Celebrate Eid
Monday, March 23rd - Pajama Day

Friday, April 3rd - Good Friday
SOCDS is Closed
Sunday, April 19th - Earth Day Reduce, Reuse, and Recycle Yard Sale (PEP Event to support Ms. Linda's Gardening Program)
Logistics
Dress Appropriately for the Weather and the playground:
Please dress your child in appropriate gear for the day.  The playground continues to be wet and soggy and boots keep our feet dry.
Morning Snack
:  Please send in a morning, and if your child is staying for enrichment please send in an afternoon snack as well.  We generally allow children to choose their food and to listen to their bodies, but if you have any particular preference for what they eat and when, please just label the containers and we will accommodate.  Please remember we are a NUT FREE environment.
Water Bottles
:  Please send in a labeled water bottle with your child each 
day.  We do make sure that they drink water throughout the day.  We have two water fountains which we use to refill the bottles (so they may come home full.)
Parking:
Please remember that Vose Avenue is an active busy street.  Please pull up on the
school side
of Vose in front of the school.  It is safest to go around the block instead of turning around in driveways.  For your child's safety please try to have them leave your car from the sidewalk side of the car.
Please do not block our neighbors driveway, even for a few minutes.
Tuition:
As I receive payments I update the statement and send you a copy.  If you do not receive those from me please let me 
know.
Hours
Morning Care 7:30-8:30am
Morning Drop off is between 8:30-9am
Afternoon Pick Up is between 3:10- 3:20pm
Extended Day 3:20-5:30/5:45pm
South Orange Country Day School
461 Vose Avenue, South Orange
973.762.6451
You are receiving this email because your child attends SOCDS.  If you no longer wish to receive these updates, please unsubscribe below.
